purpose: Language concepts, paradigms, engineering principles, pedagogy, and competency mapping for full-stack development curriculum. Documents provide the technical theory and skills-scoping layer.

terminal_objective.prerequisite: None — foundational; no prior programming knowledge required
terminal_objective.concept: Language fundamentals (types, variables, control flow, functions, scope, closures, memory, error handling) are the portable, language-agnostic substrate enabling reasoning about any general-purpose programming language.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Trace execution through unfamiliar code using first principles; debug scope and closure bugs; write pure functions with explicit error handling.
terminal_objective.market_value: Critical — deficits in fundamentals are the most common reason senior engineers fail technical interviews; prerequisite for AI-assisted coding.

educational_assets.title: Execution Trace Exercise
educational_assets.deliverable: Manually trace 5 programs line by line — predicting each variable's value at each step — before running them; compare prediction to output

educational_assets.title: Closure and Scope Debugging Lab
educational_assets.deliverable: Fix 8 broken programs where the bug is exclusively a scope or closure issue; explain each fix in writing

educational_assets.title: Pure Function Refactoring
educational_assets.deliverable: Refactor 5 functions with side effects into pure functions; document what state was moved and where

educational_assets.title: Type System Concepts
educational_assets.deliverable: 20-question quiz: static vs dynamic typing, type inference, type coercion, null vs undefined, value vs reference semantics

terminal_objective.prerequisite: language-fundamentals — must understand functions as first-class values; objects before OOP
terminal_objective.concept: Programming paradigms (imperative, OOP, functional, declarative, event-driven) are mutually compatible thinking models; modern production systems use multiple paradigms deliberately; paradigm choice is a design decision.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Identify the paradigm in any code review; justify paradigm choices in design documents; refactor imperative loops to functional pipelines; model a UI tree using functional+declarative.
terminal_objective.market_value: High — multi-paradigm literacy is table stakes for React/TypeScript (functional+declarative), Node.js (event-driven), and LLM orchestration code.

educational_assets.title: Paradigm Translation Lab
educational_assets.deliverable: Implement the same data transformation in imperative, OOP, and functional styles; compare readability, testability, and mutability

educational_assets.title: Code Review Paradigm Identification
educational_assets.deliverable: Annotate 10 code snippets identifying the paradigm in use and evaluating whether it is the most appropriate choice for the given problem

educational_assets.title: Multi-Paradigm Refactor
educational_assets.deliverable: Refactor a provided imperative JavaScript module to use functional pipelines (map/filter/reduce); preserve behavior; add unit tests verifying equivalence

terminal_objective.prerequisite: programming-paradigms (SOLID assumes OOP context); language-fundamentals for all
terminal_objective.concept: SE principles (DRY, YAGNI, KISS, SoC, SOLID, GoF patterns, Clean Code) are prescriptive design heuristics governing maintainability, testability, and scalability of production codebases over time.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Conduct a code review identifying principle violations; propose refactors; write a design doc justifying architectural choices against SOLID criteria.
terminal_objective.market_value: Critical at mid–senior level — SE principles are the language of architecture interviews, PR feedback, and technical design documents.

educational_assets.title: SOLID Violation Detection Lab
educational_assets.deliverable: Analyze 5 provided class designs; identify which SOLID principle(s) each violates; produce a refactored version that satisfies all five principles

educational_assets.title: Design Pattern Application
educational_assets.deliverable: Implement Observer, Strategy, and Decorator patterns in a provided skeleton; explain when each should and should not be used

educational_assets.title: Codebase Refactoring Kata
educational_assets.deliverable: Refactor a 200-line 'God Object' into SRP-compliant modules using DRY, SoC, and at least one GoF pattern; justify every structural decision

educational_assets.title: Clean Code PR Review Rubric
educational_assets.deliverable: Use the Clean Code rubric to evaluate a provided pull request; score naming, function length, comment quality, and error handling; write actionable feedback

terminal_objective.concept: Programming pedagogy is the research discipline covering how to teach programming; evidence-based strategies (notional machine, PRIMM, faded worked examples, Parsons Problems, Peer Instruction, live coding) are proven superior to passive instruction.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Re-sequence any programming lesson using PRIMM; replace all early blank-slate labs with faded worked examples; create a Parsons Problem for every new algorithmic construct.
terminal_objective.market_value: High for ed-tech and curriculum dev — translates to measurable outcomes; relevant for LLM tutors requiring pedagogically sound prompt designs.

educational_assets.title: PRIMM Lesson Redesign
educational_assets.deliverable: Take a provided 'traditional' programming lesson and restructure it into PRIMM format with a code prediction task, run phase, three investigation questions, a modification challenge, and a make task

educational_assets.title: Parsons Problem Authoring Lab
educational_assets.deliverable: Create three Parsons Problems for different algorithm types (loop, recursion, async); include distractors; write a difficulty justification for each

educational_assets.title: Notional Machine Visualization
educational_assets.deliverable: Draw the memory state (stack + heap) at each step of three JavaScript programs including one with a closure; verify against Python Tutor

educational_assets.title: Anti-Pattern Recognition Quiz
educational_assets.deliverable: 12 teaching scenarios — identify which programming pedagogy anti-pattern each represents and propose the evidence-based intervention

terminal_objective.prerequisite: None — this is the curriculum scoping reference used to map content to employment targets
terminal_objective.concept: Full-stack competency is a 7-tier progression (Tier 0 foundations → Tier 6 professional skills) covering frontend, backend, DevOps, and professional engineering practices; tiers are prerequisites for each other.
terminal_objective.practical_application: Use to: assess learner placement tier, identify curriculum gaps against a target role, prioritize next topics to teach, and scope capstone project requirements.
terminal_objective.market_value: Critical — maps directly to 2025–2026 hiring criteria; Tier 0–3 = entry-level ($65–95k); Tier 4–5 = mid-level ($95–130k); Tier 6 = senior ($130k+).

educational_assets.title: Learner Tier Placement Assessment
educational_assets.deliverable: Self-assessment rubric spanning all 7 tiers; learner rates each competency against proficiency levels; produces a gap map and recommended next steps

educational_assets.title: Curriculum Gap Mapping Sprint
educational_assets.deliverable: Compare a provided course syllabus against the competency tiers; produce a written gap report identifying missing competencies, missequenced tiers, and recommended additions

educational_assets.title: Portfolio Tier Evaluation Rubric
educational_assets.deliverable: Evaluate a provided GitHub portfolio against Tier 1–5 criteria; assign placement tier; produce written feedback with specific missing evidence
